mechanism_narrative: The level of the effective federal funds rate negatively predicts S&P 500 forward 20-day returns because higher risk-free rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, reducing present equity valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day forward returns is positive or |IC| < 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point or greater increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor income and consumption demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and retail sales amplify recession risk beyond their individual effects, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns through collapsing final demand and inventory destocking.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and RSAFS does not add incremental explanatory power (F-test p > 0.05) beyond main effects for 60-day returns, or the predicted sign is positive.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a federal funds rate above 4.5% signals restrictive short-term liquidity that compresses equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns as financing costs bite.
failure_condition: In periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the average 5-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 percent signals negative supply shocks and falling capacity utilization, leading to negative S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of months when INDPRO declines exceed 0.5 percent, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Higher year-over-year growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects stronger nominal aggregate demand and corporate revenue expansion, positively predicting S&P 500 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS growth and 20-day forward returns is negative or absolute IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly rising unemployment and falling payroll employment signal a simultaneous contraction in labor market slack and income generation, predicting negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns beyond either variable alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS changes fails to significantly improve prediction (F-test p > 0.05) versus main effects for 60-day returns, or the sign is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.25 million units signals a collapsing residential investment sector that drags down construction, employment, and related equities, producing negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1250, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in more than 50 percent of instances, or the mean conditional return is not significantly negative.
